arms.description: pemetrexed 500mg/m2 + carboplatin AUC5 or pemetrexed 500mg/m2 + cisplatin 75mg/m2
arms.label: Platinum-based doublet chemotherapy

brief_title: AZD9291 (Osimertinib) Versus Platinum-Based Doublet-Chemotherapy in Locally Advanced or Metastatic Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
keywords: Advanced Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer; T790M Mutation Positive
official_title: A Phase III, Open Label, Randomized Study of AZD9291 Versus Platinum-Based Doublet Chemotherapy for Patients With Locally Advanced or Metastatic Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Whose Disease Has Progressed With Previous Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor Tyrosine Kinase Inhibitor Therapy and Whose Tumours Harbour a T790M Mutation Within the Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor Gene (AURA3).

primary_outcomes.description: Per Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumours (RECIST v1.1) assessed by MRI or CT: Progressive Disease (PD): \>= 20% increase in the sum of diameters of TLs and an absolute increase in sum of diameters of \>=5mm (compared to the previous minimum sum) or progression of NTLs or a new lesion. PFS is the time from date of randomisation until the date of PD (by investigator assessment) or death (by any cause in the absence of progression) regardless of whether the patient withdrew from randomised therapy or received another anti-cancer therapy prior to progression. Patients who had not progr...
primary_outcomes.measure: Progression Free Survival (PFS) by Investigator Assessment
primary_outcomes.time_frame: RECIST tumour assessments every 6 weeks from randomisation until objective disease progression up to 19 months (at the time of the primary PFS analysis).

secondary_outcomes.measure: Objective Response Rate (ORR) by Investigator Assessment
secondary_outcomes.time_frame: RECIST tumour assessments every 6 weeks from randomisation until objective disease progression up to 19 months (at the time of the primary PFS analysis).

secondary_outcomes.measure: Duration of Response (DoR) by Investigator Assessment
secondary_outcomes.time_frame: RECIST tumour assessments every 6 weeks from randomisation until objective disease progression up to 19 months (at the time of the primary PFS analysis).

secondary_outcomes.measure: Disease Control Rate (DCR) by Investigator Assessment
